L1.area: Server-side API endpoint handlers and request processing

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where insufficient validation of user-supplied file paths allows an attacker to bypass directory traversal protections. By using OS-specific path representations in HTTP requests, an attacker can write arbitrary files to locations outside the intended directory on the server.

L3.area: Server-side API endpoint handlers and request processing
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where insufficient validation of user-supplied file paths allows an attacker to bypass directory traversal protections. By using OS-specific path representations in HTTP requests, an attacker can write arbitrary files to locations outside the intended directory on the server.

ground_truth.reason: The server's path validation function fails to reject Windows drive-relative paths, allowing an attacker to PUT arbitrary files outside the intended artifact directory via crafted path parameters.
